# Midnight

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Solar Midnight

Solar midnight is the Sun’s lower transit at the end of the day being calculated. It may occur after clock midnight; for example, for February 8 it refers to the midnight between February 8 and February 9. It is when the Sun is [transiting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transit_%28astronomy%29) the lower [celestial meridian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meridian_%28astronomy%29), or when the Sun is at its [nadir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nadir).

Using the NOAA calculator, this is calculated as astronomical midnight. See [The Definition of Chatzos](https://kosherjava.com/2020/07/02/definition-of-chatzos/) for details on the definition of solar noon and midday.

With the NOAA calculator, this time is never expected to be unavailable.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `AstronomicalCalendar.getSolarMidnight`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.AstronomicalCalendar.getSolarMidnight`

## Chatzos Halayla

Chatzos halayla is the midnight point at the end of the day being calculated, and it may actually be after clock midnight. For example, when calculating it for Erev Pesach, it is calculated for Lail Pesach so it can be used as the zman for sof zman achilas chametz.

Using the NOAA calculator, it is calculated more accurately as astronomical chatzos halayla when astronomical chatzos is enabled. If astronomical chatzos is not enabled, it is calculated as the halfway point between sunset and the following day’s sunrise. See [The Definition of Chatzos](https://kosherjava.com/2020/07/02/definition-of-chatzos/) for a detailed explanation of ways to calculate chatzos.

This time depends on whether astronomical chatzos is enabled.

The halfway-between-sunset-and-next-sunrise calculation may not be available in the Arctic on a day when either sunset or the following day’s sunrise did not happen. With the NOAA calculator, astronomical chatzos halayla can still be calculated in that situation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getChatzosHalayla`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getChatzosHalayla`
